title: Ophthalmic Medical Technicians
description: Assist ophthalmologists by performing ophthalmic clinical functions. May administer eye exams, administer eye medications, and instruct the patient in care and use of corrective lenses.

tasks.text: Take and document patients' medical histories.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Conduct tonometry or tonography tests to measure intraocular pressure.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Operate ophthalmic equipment, such as autorefractors, phoropters, tomographs, or retinoscopes.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Take anatomical or functional ocular measurements of the eye or surrounding tissue, such as axial length measurements.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Measure visual acuity, including near, distance, pinhole, or dynamic visual acuity, using appropriate tests.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Measure and record lens power, using lensometers.
tasks.rationale: Routine cognitive or manual task with high vulnerability to automation.

tasks.text: Administer topical ophthalmic or oral medications.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Conduct visual field tests to measure field of vision.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Assist physicians in performing ophthalmic procedures, including surgery.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Measure corneal curvature with keratometers or ophthalmometers to aid in the diagnosis of conditions, such as astigmatism.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Conduct ocular motility tests to measure function of eye muscles.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Clean or sterilize ophthalmic or surgical instruments.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Maintain ophthalmic instruments or equipment.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Instruct patients in the care and use of contact lenses.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Assess refractive conditions of eyes, using retinoscopes.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Call patients to inquire about their post-operative status or recovery.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Assist patients to insert or remove contact lenses.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Conduct binocular disparity tests to assess depth perception.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Adjust or make minor repairs to spectacles or eyeglasses.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Assist patients to select eyewear.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.
